codeReview.summary: The fix directly removes the unsound ExprIf and ExprMatch branch-sampling that the prior review flagged in lib/exhaust.ml and replaces it with authoritative-only enum propagation. The remaining inference paths are limited to identifiers already in the type environment, direct enum paths, function return types, and block final expressions, so let-bound if/match results are no longer cached under a guessed enum. The added regression tests cover both corrected behavior (no bogus enum caching for unannotated if/match initializers) and preserved behavior (annotated whole-expression enum values still trigger exhaustiveness checks, while pattern-based fallback still catches non-exhaustive matches when patterns reveal the enum). I did not find an obvious regression in the reviewed scope.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, prior failed review, and commit 1b82807. The fix adequately addresses the earlier unsound whole-expression enum inference by eliminating ExprIf/ExprMatch sampling and adds focused regression coverage showing unannotated multi-branch expressions no longer poison exhaustiveness type propagation while annotated cases still behave correctly.
